content:
I don't see any patches on the ML. Is it safe to assume no one has worked on this yet?

@Asahi Lina Hi Lina. My colleague Boris Brezillion and I are a bit confused on how you wrote Asahi without any IRQ support apparently. How is that so?

the irqs are abstracted away into apple-rtk, the mailbox like interface the coprocessors and AP use for their shared memory communication system
apple-rtk is implemented in C because it is used by other drivers and was there long before the asahi driver
did you find or write a request_irq?

@Dave Airlie yeah I wrote one

https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/merge_requests/12
Cheating a bit by asking for an early review from Alice lol
Not sure if this is what you are looking for, but the nvme tree has support for registering irq handlers.
It is actually also present in the old pre-merge rust branch. But the version in the nvme tree is rebased to latest upstream.

@Andreas Hindborg yes, but I am working on a version that uses Opaque<T> and PinInit instead of ForeignOwnable. It also does away with this InternalRegistration type and all the Irq chip stuff, which I do not really care about.
Cool, looking forward to trying it out :+1:
JFYI too: sending out a new version of the spinlockirq work today hopefully, which also introduces a LocalInterruptDisabled token y'all might be interested in :)

@Lyude ack, thanks for that, we really need it :D

will send a new version for IRQ once your patch is out
sure thing, should be doing it in just a moment (ended up taking a little longer while we resolved some kunit trouble)
